executive_summary: The Strait of Hormuz emerges as the most critical geopolitical flashpoint, with RUSI analysis highlighting the complex challenges of securing this vital oil chokepoint amid escalating US-Iran tensions ahead of a ceasefire deadline. Sri Lanka continues its fragile political reset process, while UN reform discussions focus on selecting a successor to strengthen multilateral governance amid rising global tensions.

key_insights.insight: RUSI analysis warns of the reality and complexities involved in an extended closure of the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most critical oil shipping chokepoints
US crude futures decline as investors reassess risks surrounding US-Iran tensions ahead of an approaching ceasefire deadline
Analysis of what 'securing' the Strait of Hormuz actually requires in terms of military and diplomatic resources
Sri Lanka continues its bumpy political reset journey following the 2022 economic crisis, facing ongoing governance challenges
Crisis Group outlines priorities for the UN regarding what to ask of the next Secretary-General to strengthen multilateral diplomacy

blind_spot: The growing strategic competition between the US and China in the Gulf region, including Chinese investments in regional infrastructure and naval presence, remains underreported despite its long-term implications for Hormuz security and global energy markets.
